promote.desc: Total number of people affected by a natural disaster.
promote.source: EM-DAT, CRED / UCLouvain (2026) – with major processing by Our World in Data
promote.reason: Droughts and cyclones regularly impact millions of people, making exposure a key vulnerability.

promote.label: Access to safely managed drinking water
promote.desc: Proportion of people using an improved drinking water source that is located on premises, available when needed and free from faecal and priority chemical contamination.
promote.source: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply, Sanitation and Hygiene (2025) – processed by Our World in Data
promote.reason: Zimbabwe has seen a highly unusual and critical decline in clean water access since 2000.

exclude.reason: The official unemployment rate of ~9% is highly misleading in Zimbabwe, where almost the entire labor force works in informal jobs to survive

promote.label: Share of workers in informal employment
promote.desc: Share of employed people working in jobs that lack basic social or legal protection and employment benefits. Does not include illegal and illicit activities. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.
promote.source: International Labour Organization – with major processing by Our World in Data
promote.reason: Informal employment is the defining feature of Zimbabwe's labor market, encompassing nearly 90% of all workers

promote.label: Agricultural productivity per worker
promote.source: World Bank (World Development Indicators) and ILO (ILOSTAT) (2025) – processed by Our World in Data
promote.reason: Stagnating yields and economic shifts have led to an unusual long-term decline in productivity per worker since 1991.

exclude.reason: Minor, stable metric that is secondary to Zimbabwe's pressing infrastructure and development challenges

promote.label: Share of people who say most people can be trusted
promote.desc: % of respondents replying "Most people can be trusted" when asked "Generally speaking, would you say that most people can be trusted or that you need to be very careful in dealing with people?" Possible answers include "Most people can be trusted", "Do not know" and "Need to be very careful".
promote.source: Integrated Values Surveys (2024) – with major processing by Our World in Data
promote.reason: Interpersonal trust has collapsed to one of the lowest levels globally (just 2%) amidst decades of economic and political instability.

exclude.reason: Terrorism deaths have been consistently zero throughout the entire period.
Zimbabwe has only launched one satellite, making annual space launches an uninformative metric flat at 1
The annual number of clinical trials is extremely low (1 to 2 trials), making it unrepresentative of the country's technology landscape
